summary: Excellent soundboard recording captures a blistering 4/5/71 show with Pigpen‑driven Lovelight, a scorching Cold Rain opener and a monster Truckin > Other One sequence.
review: The board‑tape is crisp and clean, delivering the full power of what many fans call one of the tightest 1971 performances.  Highlights include a blazing Cold Rain start, Pigpen’s incendiary King Bee and Lovelight, and an explosive Truckin > The Other One medley that feels like the “Skull & Roses” sequence in its raw form.  Jerry’s guitar work (even yodeling on Bobby McGee) and Phil’s thunderous bass round out a show that most reviewers deem essential.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is 4.5/5 with the vast majority praising both performance and sound quality; only a single outlier gives a low score, so confidence in a strong positive rating is high.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean board mix with no reported drop‑outs or muddiness; reviewers repeatedly call the sound ‘incredible’ and a must‑have for Deadheads.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show opens with a high‑energy Cold Rain, builds through Pigpen’s stellar King Bee and Lovelight, then erupts in the second‑set Truckin > Other One medley that captures the classic 1969‑ish intensity; the pacing stays relentless from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia delivers unforgettable guitar work, highlighted by a playful yodel on Bobby McGee and sustained brilliance throughout the second set.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is thunderous, especially during Lovelight and a standout ‘Philbomb’ solo that “brings the house down.”
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s vocals are noted as distinct on the NFA/Skull‑&‑Roses segment; otherwise no specific comments.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann (drums) is praised for ‘killing it’, providing powerful drive especially in Deal.
